domain: Sardinian cantu a tenore (Barbagia region 4-male-singer polyphony with bassu+contra+boche+mesu-boche; bassu/contra use overtone-rich phonation; QUINTINA = emergent 5th 'phantom' voice acoustically arising from 4-voice overtone combination)
specific_mechanism: Spectral-allocation scheme with K specialist heads each constrained to a distinct RoPE frequency band, plus a (K+1)-th 'phantom' channel computed as Hadamard products of adjacent K-head outputs (overtone analog) — phantom has no own weights, fully determined by combined overtones of the K heads.
llm_application: TENORES-QUINTINA: K-head spectral-allocation + emergent (K+1)-th phantom channel via cross-band Hadamard products. Architecture: (1) K attention heads each restricted to a distinct RoPE-frequency band B_k (cf. Mixed-Frequency RoPE); (2) compute O_k = head_k(Q, K, V) standard outputs; (3) compute phantom channel O_{K+1} = sum over adjacent (i,j) pairs of (O_i ⊙ O_j) (Hadamard product of adjacent-band outputs) — no learnable weights for phantom, emerges from combined K-head overtones; (4) final feature = concat[O_1..O_K, O_{K+1}]·W_out. Phantom channel captures cross-frequency-band interaction analogous to quintina overtone fifth-voice in cantu a tenore. Differs from R416 XALAM-FREQ (per-head fixed band but no phantom emergence), R402 TUVAN-IGIL (2-head sympathetic ghost head via shared subspace, not adjacent-band Hadamard product), DDSP 2306.17252 (signal-processing layer, not attention), Additive Synthesis (waveform construction, not attention features), Chord-Aware Synth (selected-harmonic combination at audio layer).
novelty_claim: Mixed-Frequency RoPE + R402 ghost-head + DDSP + Additive Synth cover per-head spectral specialization + cross-feature combination space. TENORES-QUINTINA adds explicit EMERGENT phantom channel as Hadamard product of adjacent-band outputs (zero-weight emergence). Recombination — adjacent-product features are well-established in CNNs and feature crosses.
